qa.user_verbatim_complaint: Four interacting privilege escalation bugs in /allow sentinel grant mechanism: (1) _looks_regex() classifies dotted paths as regex, skipping sentinel write for --tool flag; (2) bare /allow Write /path form treats path as comment token, producing wildcard sentinel; (3) writer emits args_contain but matcher keys on target, causing wildcard fallthrough; (4) match_sentinel_grant_for_write uses .get('target') returning None which wildcard-matches any entry without target field. Together these allowed path-scoped /allow grants to silently become global Write grants.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Codex independently confirmed that /allow --tool Write /tmp/file.json (unquoted, no quotes around the combined Write+path) still produces a bare wildcard sentinel {op: Write} with no target field. Live verification confirmed this: sentinel has op=Write but no target. This is Edge Case 5 in the BA spec (ticket-20260524-125300-B.md) and explicitly marked OUT OF SCOPE. The correct syntax is /allow --tool "Write /path" (quoted) or /allow Write /path (bare two-token form). Pre-existing documented limitation, not a regression introduced by this cycle.
out_of_scope_observations.follow_on_recommendation: Consider adding detection for the 3-token bare form (/allow Write /path where --tool is absent but first bare token is Write and second bare token is a path) to cover users who accidentally omit --tool. Currently this falls into unquoted 3-token bare detection which does NOT produce a scoped sentinel.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: AC3 test calls consume_sentinel_grant_on_terminal_result(task_id, 'success') directly from the library — it does NOT invoke posttool-allowlist-consume.py main() as a subprocess. The AC spec text says 'posttool-allowlist-consume.py main() runs'. This is a spec_text_vs_execution_drift (Dimension 5): the test verifies the correct behavioral outcome (sentinel unlinked) via the library function, but not the full hook executable path. The behavioral assertion is correct. Codex independently confirmed consume_sentinel_grant_on_terminal_result works correctly. BA should update AC3's verification description to either: (a) accept library-level testing as the canonical verification path, or (b) require a subprocess invocation of posttool-allowlist-consume.py.
out_of_scope_observations.follow_on_recommendation: BA should update AC3 check field from 'posttool-allowlist-consume.py main() via ...' to explicitly state whether library-level testing is acceptable or subprocess invocation is required.

qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: All 4 bugs fixed in source code and confirmed by direct inspection: (1) is_regex=False at line 91 of userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh (force literal for --tool flag); (2) bare Write+path branch at lines 98-103 with no double-quotes in embedded Python comments; (3) sentinel writer emits target for Write ops, args_contain for others at lines 213-218; (4) key-presence branching in match_sentinel_grant_for_write at lines 525-536 of allowlist.py. All 54 tests pass including AC5 (no wildcard leakage) and AC7/AC8 (E2E subprocess regression guards).

complaint_element_pointer.how_located: task-id-anchored: 20260524-125300-B == cycle scope; files listed in dev.files_modified

qa.overall_assessment: PASS. All 4 privilege escalation bugs fixed and verified by direct source inspection plus 54 passing tests including adversarial security regressions. Codex consultation completed (codex_required: true); 1 finding classified out-of-scope per BA spec Edge Case 5; 1 spec_text_vs_execution_drift observation surfaced for AC3 test depth (behavioral outcome still correct). No band-aid patterns detected. Provenance confirmed for all 3 modified files.

success_criteria_results.criterion: match_sentinel_grant_for_write(task_id, session_id, '/tmp/allowed.json') returns a non-None entry when sentinel contains {op: Write, target: /tmp/allowed.json}
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Direct source inspection of match_sentinel_grant_for_write key-presence branching (lines 525-536 of allowlist.py) plus pytest execution: test_AC1 and test_match_returns_entry (pre-existing) both pass
success_criteria_results.details: if 'target' in entry branch: t == target_path -> return entry. Entry with target=/tmp/allowed.json matches query for /tmp/allowed.json. Test passes.
success_criteria_results.evidence: pytest confirmed pass; source code at hooks/lib/allowlist.py:525-536

success_criteria_results.criterion: match_sentinel_grant_for_write(task_id, session_id, '/tmp/other.json') returns None when sentinel contains {op: Write, target: /tmp/allowed.json}
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Direct source inspection: t == target_path branch returns entry only on exact match; mismatch falls through to return None (implicit). Test test_AC2 and test_target_mismatch_returns_none both pass. Also verified adversarially: ran Python inline test confirming None returned for mismatched path.
success_criteria_results.details: Exact string equality enforced: 'target' in entry, t == target_path is False for /tmp/other.json, falls to end of loop, returns None. Critically: deny-by-default (fail-closed) — no fallthrough to wildcard when target is present but wrong.
success_criteria_results.evidence: pytest confirmed pass; adversarial inline security test confirmed None returned

success_criteria_results.criterion: After posttool-allowlist-consume.py main() runs for Write PostToolUse with tool_input.file_path matching sentinel target, sentinel file is unlinked
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Test test_AC3 calls consume_sentinel_grant_on_terminal_result(task_id, 'success') directly from library and asserts sentinel file is unlinked. Behavioral outcome (unlink) verified. Note: test uses library function, not subprocess invocation of main() — spec_text_vs_execution_drift flagged as out-of-scope observation.
success_criteria_results.details: consume_sentinel_grant_on_terminal_result returns True and os.path.exists(path) is False after call. Test passes. Codex independently confirmed the consume function works correctly via its own subprocess testing.
success_criteria_results.evidence: pytest confirmed test_AC3 pass; Codex subprocess verification confirmed consume behavior

success_criteria_results.criterion: match_sentinel_grant_for_write returns None when entry uses legacy args_contain schema with a different path
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Direct source inspection of elif 'args_contain' in entry branch (lines 529-533): exact equality check ac[0] == target_path enforced. Test test_ac4_legacy_args_contain_denies_other_path and test_AC4 both pass.
success_criteria_results.details: Legacy args_contain branch: ac[0] == target_path is exact equality. args_contain=['/tmp/allowed.json'] does not match query for '/tmp/evil.json'. Returns None (deny).
success_criteria_results.evidence: pytest confirmed pass for both test_AC4 and test_ac4_legacy_args_contain_denies_other_path

success_criteria_results.criterion: No wildcard leakage: args_contain entry with path /tmp/al does NOT match query for /tmp/allowed.json (prefix substring exploit blocked)
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Direct source inspection: exact equality ac[0] == target_path (not startswith/in). Test test_ac5_exploit_shape_regression_no_wildcard_leakage and test_AC5 both pass. Inline adversarial verification run: ac[0]='/tmp/al', query='/tmp/allowed.json' -> None confirmed.
success_criteria_results.details: '/tmp/al' == '/tmp/allowed.json' is False -> returns None. Prefix, suffix, and substring attacks all blocked by exact equality.
success_criteria_results.evidence: pytest confirmed pass; adversarial inline security test confirmed None for prefix exploit

success_criteria_results.criterion: Intentional bare {op: Write} entry (no target, no args_contain) returns non-None for any target path (wildcard behavior preserved)
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Direct source inspection of else branch (line 534-536): if neither 'target' nor 'args_contain' is present, return entry (wildcard). Test test_ac6_bare_write_sentinel_wildcard_preserved and test_AC6 both pass.
success_criteria_results.details: else: return entry — bare {op: Write} intentionally matches any path. Preserves backward compatibility for /allow Write (no path restriction) syntax.
success_criteria_results.evidence: pytest confirmed pass; source code at hooks/lib/allowlist.py:534-536

success_criteria_results.criterion: E2E: hook invoked with /allow --tool "Write /tmp/file.json" produces sentinel with op==Write and target==/tmp/file.json (not wildcard, not missing)
success_criteria_results.verification_method: E2E subprocess test test_AC7 invokes actual bash hook binary with JSON payload via subprocess. Asserts sentinel exists, entry has op==Write and target==/tmp/file.json, no args_contain field. Also asserts match for /tmp/file.json returns non-None and match for /tmp/other.json returns None. Tests test_ac7_e2e_tool_flag_dotted_path_sentinel and test_AC7 both pass.
success_criteria_results.details: Critical regression guard: BUG1 (_looks_regex misclassification) and BUG3 (args_contain vs target) both caught by this E2E test. Hook produces correct scoped sentinel. Dotted filename /tmp/file.json correctly treated as literal (is_regex=False forced).
success_criteria_results.evidence: pytest confirmed pass for both test_AC7 and test_ac7_e2e_tool_flag_dotted_path_sentinel; E2E subprocess invocation of actual hook binary

success_criteria_results.criterion: E2E: hook invoked with bare /allow Write /tmp/file.json (no --tool) produces sentinel with op==Write and target==/tmp/file.json (not wildcard)
success_criteria_results.verification_method: E2E subprocess test test_AC8 invokes actual bash hook binary with bare-syntax JSON payload. Asserts sentinel exists, entry has op==Write and target==/tmp/file.json, no args_contain field. Also asserts match/no-match for correct/wrong paths. Tests test_ac8_e2e_bare_syntax_scoped_sentinel and test_AC8 both pass.
success_criteria_results.details: Critical regression guard: BUG2 (bare Write+path treated as comment) and BUG3 (args_contain vs target) both caught by this E2E test. Hook produces correct scoped sentinel from bare two-token form.
success_criteria_results.evidence: pytest confirmed pass for both test_AC8 and test_ac8_e2e_bare_syntax_scoped_sentinel; E2E subprocess invocation of actual hook binary

root_cause_verification.rationale: All four root causes addressed in distinct code locations: (1) is_regex forced False at line 91 of userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh for --tool branch — no longer delegated to _looks_regex() heuristic; (2) bare Write+path branch added at lines 98-103 with correct pattern assignment and no double-quote characters in embedded Python comments; (3) writer conditionally emits 'target' for Write ops and 'args_contain' for others at lines 213-218; (4) matcher uses key-presence branching ('target' in entry / 'args_contain' in entry / else wildcard) rather than .get('target') which silently returned None. E2E tests AC7 and AC8 provide the strongest regression evidence — they invoke the actual bash hook binary and confirm the full pipeline produces correct scoped sentinels.

build_verification.skip_reason: No compiled/deployed artifacts modified. Changes are bash hook scripts and Python library modules executed at runtime by the hook infrastructure. No build step exists; correctness verified by pytest and direct source inspection.

regression_test_results.details: 54 tests passed in 0.69s. Pre-existing tests (test_match_returns_entry, test_target_mismatch_returns_none, test_targetless_wildcard_matches_any_path) all still pass — no regressions in prior behavior. New AC4-AC8 methods and all 8 generated test files pass.

All 3 files in dev.files_modified (hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh, hooks/lib/allowlist.py, hooks/tests/test_allowlist_consolidation.py) appear in git diff output. All 8 files in dev.files_created exist on disk in tests/generated/20260524-125300-B/. No dev_provenance_violation found.

Ran inline Python tests confirming: (1) args_contain sentinel with /tmp/allowed.json denies /tmp/evil.json; (2) prefix exploit /tmp/al does not match /tmp/allowed.json; (3) session mismatch enforced (wrong session_id returns None); (4) wildcard (bare op) matches any path correctly.

Codex ran pytest independently (54 passed), ran live E2E probes via pretool-write-guard.sh for quoted forms, and verified posttool consume behavior. Codex confirmed all 4 code fixes are correct in their implementation.

all_findings.issue: AC3 test verifies consume_sentinel_grant_on_terminal_result directly via library import instead of invoking posttool-allowlist-consume.py main() as a subprocess. The AC spec text says 'posttool-allowlist-consume.py main() runs'. The behavioral outcome (sentinel unlinked) is correctly verified, but the full hook executable path is not tested. This is a spec_text_vs_execution_drift (Dimension 5 objection surfaced as out-of-scope observation since behavioral assertion passes).
all_findings.recommendation: BA should update AC3 check field to explicitly state whether library-level testing is acceptable or subprocess invocation is required. If subprocess depth is required, add a subprocess test for posttool-allowlist-consume.py alongside the current library test.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex ran pytest independently (54 passed). Raised 4 findings: (1) BLOCKER: /allow --tool Write /tmp/file.json (unquoted 3-token form) produces wildcard sentinel — confirmed in live test. PROPOSED_FIX: detect 3-token bare form in userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh. (2) MAJOR: AC3 test calls library function directly instead of posttool-allowlist-consume.py main() subprocess — spec says main() runs. PROPOSED_FIX: add subprocess test for posttool main(). (3) TEST_GAP: no pretool-write-guard.sh integration test to ensure guard correctly uses the new sentinel shape. PROPOSED_FIX: add integration test for write-guard hook. Codex itself ran write-guard probes and confirmed correct behavior for quoted syntax. (4) OBSERVATION_ONLY: relative paths like ../relative not tested — marked out of scope per BA spec edge case 7.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Codex Finding 1 classified OUT_OF_SCOPE: the unquoted 3-token form /allow --tool Write /path is explicitly documented as Edge Case 5 in ticket-20260524-125300-B.md and marked out of scope. The pre-existing documented limitation predates this cycle. Surfaced as out_of_scope_observation for follow-on work. Codex Finding 2 recorded as minor finding (spec_text_vs_execution_drift) and out_of_scope_observation for BA to update AC3 verification text. Behavioral outcome still correctly verified by current test. Codex Finding 3 noted: Codex itself verified write-guard subprocess behavior; ACs do not require write-guard subprocess test; recorded as observation only. Codex Finding 4 accepted as OBSERVATION_ONLY per BA spec edge case 7 out-of-scope designation. Verdict unchanged: PASS.
